title: buddleja grandiflora var. genuina
intro: Buddleja grandiflora var. genuina, also known as the large-flowered butterfly bush, is a deciduous shrub native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its long, conical panicles of small, fragrant flowers in shades of purple, pink, and white. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Buddleja grandiflora var. genuina include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to act as a natural diuretic, promoting urinary health. Herbal preparations of Buddleja grandiflora var. genuina include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
